content:
# Deuteronomy Chapter 10 — SOPHIA Consensus Translation
**Arbiter:** SOPHIA | **Sources:** EMET (literal), LOGOS (formal), PNEUMA (dynamic) | **Leningrad Codex:** Folios 207–208 | **Vaticanus:** p. 150

---

## Council Notes

**Chapter Overview:** Chapter 10 moves from the record of sin and intercession (ch.9) to restoration and renewed calling. Structure: (1) The second tablets and the ark (vv.1–5); (2) Brief itinerary note: Aaron's death, Levitical appointment (vv.6–9, a third-person parenthesis); (3) Moses' final intercession bears fruit (v.10); (4) Renewed command to march (v.11); (5) The "what does the LORD require?" summary (v.12); (6) God's cosmic sovereignty and covenant election (vv.14–15); (7) The command to circumcise the heart (v.16); (8) God's character: great, just, lover of the sojourner (vv.17–19); (9) Exhortation to cling, fear, serve, swear by the LORD (vv.20–22).

**Key Rulings:**
- **V.1 — "Hew/Carve/Cut" tablets:** EMET: "Hew"; LOGOS: "Cut"; PNEUMA: "Carve." Hebrew pesal = hew/carve stone. Consensus: "Carve" — most natural English for stone tablet work.
- **V.4 — Aseret ha-Devarim:** EMET: "the Ten Words"; LOGOS: "the Ten Commandments"; PNEUMA: "the Ten Commandments." Consensus: "the Ten Commandments" — the established English rendering, though "ten words" is more literal. Majority (LOGOS/PNEUMA) plus established English convention.
- **V.6–9 — Itinerary parenthesis:** All three agents enclose vv.6–9 in parentheses. Retained in consensus. This is a recognized narrative inset.
- **V.8 — "minister to him and to bless in his name":** EMET: "to minister to him and to bless in his name"; LOGOS: "to minister to him and to bless in his name"; PNEUMA: "to minister to him, and to pronounce blessings in his name." Consensus: "to minister to him and to bless in his name."
- **V.9 — "the LORD is his inheritance":** All three agree. Retained.
- **V.10 — "I stayed on the mountain as the first days":** EMET: "I stayed on the mountain as the first days"; LOGOS: "I myself stayed on the mountain, as at the first time"; PNEUMA: "As for me — I stayed on the mountain as I had before." Consensus: "I myself stayed on the mountain, as at the first time."
- **V.12 — "fear...walk...love...serve...keep":** All three preserve the five-element call. PNEUMA renders yir'ah as "reverent awe"; EMET/LOGOS as "fear." Consensus: "fear" — preserves the Hebrew yir'ah which encompasses both terror and reverence; "reverent awe" is a gloss.
- **V.14 — "heaven of heavens":** EMET: "the heaven of heavens"; LOGOS: "the heaven of heavens"; PNEUMA: "even the highest heavens." Consensus: "the heaven of heavens" — literal to shemei hashamayim.
- **V.16 — "circumcise the foreskin of your heart":** EMET: "circumcise the foreskin of your heart"; LOGOS: "Circumcise therefore the foreskin of your heart"; PNEUMA: "Circumcise your hearts." Consensus: "Circumcise therefore the foreskin of your heart" — preserving the anatomical specificity of orlah (foreskin) which the LXX drops.
- **V.17 — "does not lift up faces / does not take bribes":** EMET: "who does not lift up faces and does not take bribes"; LOGOS: "who is not partial and takes no bribe"; PNEUMA: "who shows no partiality and takes no bribes." Consensus: "who shows no partiality and takes no bribe" — splitting the idiom: lo yissa panim = shows no partiality; lo yiqqach shochad = takes no bribe.
- **V.18 — "orphan" vs. "fatherless":** EMET: "the orphan"; LOGOS: "the fatherless"; PNEUMA: "the fatherless." Consensus: "the fatherless" — Hebrew yatom = fatherless/orphan; "fatherless" is the more established rendering in this context.
- **V.20 — "cling" vs. "hold fast":** EMET: "cling"; LOGOS: "hold fast"; PNEUMA: "Cling." Consensus: "cling" — Hebrew dabaq = cling/cleave (the same root as Gen. 2:24). "Cling" preserves the relational intimacy.
- **V.21 — "your praise" vs. "your glory":** EMET: "He is your praise"; LOGOS: "He is your praise"; PNEUMA: "He is your glory." Consensus: "He is your praise" — Hebrew tehillatekha = your praise/song of praise. "Glory" (PNEUMA) is a different Hebrew term (kavod).

---

## Consensus Translation — Deuteronomy 10

**1** At that time the LORD said to me, "Carve for yourself two tablets of stone like the first, and come up to me on the mountain, and also make for yourself an ark of wood.

**2** And I will write on the tablets the words that were on the first tablets, which you shattered, and you shall put them in the ark."

**3** So I made an ark of acacia wood, and carved two tablets of stone like the first, and I went up the mountain with the two tablets in my hand.

**4** And he wrote on the tablets, in the same writing as before, the Ten Commandments that the LORD had spoken to you on the mountain from the midst of the fire on the day of the assembly, and the LORD gave them to me.

**5** Then I turned and came down from the mountain and put the tablets in the ark that I had made. And there they are, as the LORD commanded me.

**(6** And the sons of Israel journeyed from Beeroth-bene-jaakan to Moserah. There Aaron died and there he was buried, and Eleazar his son ministered as priest in his place.

**7** From there they journeyed to Gudgodah, and from Gudgodah to Jotbathah, a land with streams of water.

**8** At that time the LORD set apart the tribe of Levi to carry the ark of the covenant of the LORD, to stand before the LORD to minister to him and to bless in his name, to this day.

**9** Therefore Levi has no portion or inheritance with his brothers; the LORD is his inheritance, as the LORD your God spoke to him.)

**10** I myself stayed on the mountain, as at the first time, forty days and forty nights, and the LORD listened to me that time also. The LORD was not willing to destroy you.

**11** Then the LORD said to me, "Arise, go before the people on their journey, and let them go in and possess the land that I swore to their fathers to give to them."

**12** And now, Israel, what does the LORD your God require of you, but to fear the LORD your God, to walk in all his ways and to love him, and to serve the LORD your God with all your heart and with all your soul,

**13** keeping the commandments and statutes of the LORD that I am commanding you today, for your good?

**14** Behold, to the LORD your God belong the heavens and the heaven of heavens, the earth and all that is in it.

**15** Yet the LORD set his heart in love on your fathers and chose their offspring after them — you — above all peoples, as it is this day.

**16** Circumcise therefore the foreskin of your heart, and be no longer stiff-necked.

**17** For the LORD your God is the God of gods and the Lord of lords, the great, the mighty, and the awesome God, who shows no partiality and takes no bribe —

**18** executing justice for the fatherless and the widow, and loving the sojourner, giving him food and clothing.

**19** Love the sojourner therefore, for you were sojourners in the land of Egypt.

**20** The LORD your God you shall fear; him you shall serve; and to him you shall cling; and by his name you shall swear.

**21** He is your praise and he is your God, who has done for you these great and awesome things that your eyes saw.

**22** With seventy persons your fathers went down to Egypt, and now the LORD your God has made you as numerous as the stars of the heavens.

---

## Verse-by-Verse Ruling Notes

**V.1:** "Carve" adopted as the most natural English for stone-tablet work.

**V.2:** "Shattered" (matching ch.9 ruling on vashavram) preserved for consistency.

**V.3:** "Acacia wood" — all three agree. Hebrew atzei shittim = acacia wood. Retained.

**V.4:** "The Ten Commandments" — majority ruling (LOGOS/PNEUMA). EMET's "Ten Words" is noted in textual notes but English convention favors "Ten Commandments."

**V.6–9:** Parentheses preserved. EMET: "sons of Yisra'el"; LOGOS: "people of Israel"; PNEUMA: "Israelites." Consensus: "sons of Israel" per project convention (bney yisrael).

**V.8:** "Bless in his name" — all three agree. Retained.

**V.11:** EMET: "Arise, go for the journey before the people"; LOGOS: "Arise, go on your journey before the people"; PNEUMA: "Get up, lead the people on their journey." Consensus: "Arise, go before the people on their journey" — natural and literal.

**V.12:** "Soul" for nefesh (not "life" as PNEUMA uses). Standard rendering.

**V.15:** LOGOS: "set his heart in love on" = chashaq = to be attached/delight in. Good rendering retained.

**V.16:** LOGOS preserves full "foreskin of your heart" against PNEUMA's abbreviation. MT preserved.

**V.18:** "Fatherless" (majority) over "orphan" (EMET).

**V.20:** "Cling" (EMET/PNEUMA) over "hold fast" (LOGOS). The root dabaq warrants "cling."

**V.21:** "Your praise" (EMET/LOGOS) over "your glory" (PNEUMA).

**V.22:** "As numerous as the stars of the heavens" — PNEUMA: "stars in the sky." Hebrew kekokavei hashamayim = stars of the heavens. "Heavens" (EMET/LOGOS) preferred.

---

## Textual Notes

**V.4 — Aseret ha-Devarim:** Hebrew "ten words" (devarim); LXX: δέκα λόγους (ten words/sayings). English "Ten Commandments" is an established gloss but the Hebrew literally says "words." Noted.

**V.5 — The ark:** This ark Moses makes is a simple field ark of acacia wood, distinct from the ornate Ark of the Covenant. LXX: ξύλον ἄσηπτον (imperishable wood) — describing the quality rather than the species.

**V.9 — "the LORD is his inheritance":** LXX: Κύριος αὐτὸς κλῆρος αὐτοῦ — the Greek κλῆρος means both "lot" (by casting) and "inheritance." A theologically rich statement.

**V.14 — "heaven of heavens":** LXX: ὁ οὐρανὸς καὶ ὁ οὐρανὸς τοῦ οὐρανοῦ — Hebrew superlative construction for the highest cosmic realm.

**V.16 — orlat levavkhem:** LXX: τὴν σκληροκαρδίαν ὑμῶν (hardness of heart) — the LXX replaces "foreskin" (orlah) with "hardness" (sklerokardia). MT reading preserved over LXX here; the physical concreteness of orlah is part of the theological force.

**V.20 — dabaq:** Same root as Gen. 2:24 (husband cleaving to wife). The covenantal intimacy implied by "cling" is intentional.

— SOPHIA
